source.transcription_note: Supplied as pasted Arabic text; spelling and OCR-like spacing were normalized without adding missing clinical meaning.

REF_GENERAL_LIMITS_1998.title: Drawing conclusions: a re-examination of empirical and conceptual bases for psychological evaluation of children from their drawings
REF_GENERAL_LIMITS_1998.finding: Little evidence supported reliability and validity of broad psychological assessment from children's drawings.

REF_PROJECTIVE_REVIEW_2025.title: Projective in Time: A Systematic Review on the Use of Construction Projective Techniques in the Digital Era
REF_PROJECTIVE_REVIEW_2025.finding: Recent studies had medium-low overall quality and often lacked detailed psychometric evaluation; projective methods should be supplementary.

REF_SIZE_2004.title: The effect of affective characterizations on the use of size and colour in drawings produced by children in the absence of a model
REF_SIZE_2004.finding: In an instructed task, children tended to draw positively characterized topics larger; this does not validate fixed personality thresholds.

REF_SIZE_DEPTH_2013.title: Size and emotion or depth and emotion?
REF_SIZE_DEPTH_2013.finding: Size may reflect perceived proximity/depth and is sensitive to developmental and task factors.

REF_PLACEMENT_1992.title: Children's drawings of topics differing in emotional significance: effects on placement relative to a self-drawing
REF_PLACEMENT_1992.finding: Relative placement effects were weak and easily masked; the study did not validate top/left/right personality meanings.

REF_EYES_EXPRESSION_2007.title: Expression of the emotions in the drawing of a man by the child from 5 to 11 years of age
REF_EYES_EXPRESSION_2007.finding: Children can intentionally use facial graphic cues to depict emotion; this does not establish personality inference from isolated eye style.

rules.arabic: رسم العيون الواسعة يدل على الشخصية المنفتحة.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing wide eyes indicates an outgoing personality.
rules.professional_reasoning: Wide eyes were detected, but the supplied personality interpretation is unvalidated.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing includes wide eyes. Ask the child what the expression means to them.
limitations: Eye style may reflect emotion depiction, artistic convention, copying, age, or skill.
Clinician-only hypothesis.

rules.arabic: رسم العيون الصارمة يدل على الشعور بالغضب.
rules.english: The notes propose that stern-looking eyes indicate a feeling of anger.
rules.professional_reasoning: Stern-looking eyes may be an intentional anger cue, but context and the child's explanation are required.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The eyes may have been drawn with a stern expression. Ask what the character is feeling.
limitations: An depicted angry expression is not evidence that the child is angry or aggressive.

rules.arabic: رسم العيون المغمضة يدل على رفض الشخص النظر إلى نفسه من الداخل ورفضه الاعتراف بعيوبه.
rules.english: The notes propose that closed eyes mean refusing self-reflection or acknowledgement of faults.
rules.professional_reasoning: Closed eyes were detected; the categorical interpretation is unsupported.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing includes closed eyes. Their meaning is unknown without asking the child.
limitations: Restricted from direct parent interpretation.
Many ordinary artistic explanations are possible.

rules.arabic: رسم النمر أو الذئب يدل على الشعور بالغضب.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing a tiger or wolf indicates anger.
rules.professional_reasoning: A tiger or wolf was detected; no reliable source was found for inferring the child's anger.
rules.parent_safe_wording: An animal resembling a tiger or wolf may be present. Ask why it was chosen.
limitations: Animal choice may reflect stories, interests, assignments, or cultural symbolism.

rules.arabic: رسم الثعلب يدل على أن الرسام كان يفكر في عمل أمر لئيم أو خبيث.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing a fox indicates thinking about doing something mean or malicious.
rules.professional_reasoning: A fox may be present; the malicious-intent interpretation is unsupported and must not be inferred.
rules.parent_safe_wording: A fox may be present. Ask the child about its role in the picture.
limitations: Clinician-only; categorical intent attribution is unsafe.

rules.arabic: رسم السنجاب يشير إلى حاجة الرسام للحماية والاهتمام.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing a squirrel indicates a need for protection and attention.
rules.professional_reasoning: A squirrel may be present; no validated inference about protection needs follows from it alone.
rules.parent_safe_wording: A squirrel may be present. Ask what it needs and what is happening around it.
limitations: Requires converging interview and contextual evidence.

rules.arabic: رسم الأسد يشير إلى اعتقاد الشخص بأنه متفوق على الآخرين.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing a lion indicates believing oneself superior to others.
rules.professional_reasoning: A lion may be present; superiority cannot be inferred from this symbol.
rules.parent_safe_wording: A lion may be present. Ask what the lion represents in the story.
limitations: Restricted categorical personality claim.

rules.arabic: حب رسم الأشكال الهندسية يشير إلى كثرة الأهداف والخطط والعناد والإصرار.
rules.english: The notes propose that frequently drawing geometric shapes indicates many goals and plans, stubbornness, and persistence.
rules.professional_reasoning: Repeated geometry was detected; planning or personality traits cannot be inferred without other evidence.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing uses repeated geometric forms. Ask what the pattern means.
limitations: May reflect task demands, practice, aesthetics, or motor preference.

rules.arabic: رسم النجوم يشير إلى أن الشخص يريد أن يكون ملفتاً لنظر الجميع.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing stars indicates wanting everyone's attention.
rules.professional_reasoning: Stars were detected; attention-seeking cannot be inferred from the symbol.
rules.parent_safe_wording: Stars are present. Ask what they mean in this drawing.
limitations: Weak symbolic hypothesis.

rules.arabic: رسم الزهور والسحب والشمس قد يعبر عن تخيل أهداف وأفكار جديدة وشخصية إيجابية ومزاج جيد.
rules.english: The notes propose that flowers, clouds, and sun may express imagined future goals and new ideas, positivity, and good mood.
rules.professional_reasoning: These motifs were detected, but mood and personality require contextual confirmation.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing includes positive-weather or nature motifs. Ask the child how the scene feels.
limitations: Common motifs may be decorative or prompted.

rules.arabic: رسم الدوائر يعبر عن الشعور بالوحدة والحاجة إلى شخص قريب يقف بجانبه.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing circles expresses loneliness and a need for someone close.
rules.professional_reasoning: Circles were detected; loneliness cannot be inferred from circles alone.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing contains circles. Ask what they represent and who is present or absent.
limitations: Do not display loneliness as a conclusion.

rules.arabic: رسم وسائل النقل يشير إلى حب السفر والعطلة والاجتماعية والانفتاح والمرح والمغامرة وحب الحياة.
rules.english: The notes associate vehicles with travel, holidays, sociability, openness, playfulness, adventure, and enjoyment of life.
rules.professional_reasoning: A vehicle was detected; personality traits cannot be inferred from it.
rules.parent_safe_wording: A vehicle may be present. Ask where it is going and who is travelling.
limitations: Vehicle interests and drawing prompts are alternative explanations.

rules.arabic: رسم القلوب يدل على شخصية عاطفية أو عاشقة واجتماعية ورومانسية لطيفة وقادرة على جذب قلوب الآخرين.
rules.english: The notes associate hearts with an affectionate, loving, social, gentle, romantic personality.
rules.professional_reasoning: Hearts were detected; they may conventionally express affection, but personality cannot be concluded.
rules.parent_safe_wording: Heart shapes are present. Ask who or what they are connected to.
limitations: Cultural and decorative convention is a strong alternative explanation.

rules.arabic: إذا غطت الرسمة نحو 50% من الورقة فقد تدل على الانفتاح أحياناً والانطواء أحياناً أخرى.
rules.english: The notes propose that covering about half the page indicates being outgoing at times and introverted at others.
rules.professional_reasoning: The drawing's bounding box covers about half the page. Research shows size can vary with task and emotional characterization, but this personality interpretation is not validated.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing occupies about half the page; this is a visual measurement, not a personality conclusion.
limitations: Page coverage is not equivalent to emotional or personality status.

rules.arabic: إذا غطت الرسمة كل الورقة فهذا يدل على احترام الشخص العالي لنفسه وتقديره لها.
rules.english: The notes propose that filling the whole page indicates high self-respect and self-esteem.
rules.professional_reasoning: The drawing spans nearly the full page. This may reflect planning, scale, task, or style; self-esteem cannot be concluded.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing uses nearly the whole page. Ask how the child planned the space.
limitations: No validated 90-100% threshold for self-esteem.

rules.arabic: إذا لم تتجاوز الرسمة 20% من الورقة فقد يدل ذلك على عدم الاستقرار أو الخوف.
rules.english: The notes propose that using no more than 20% of the page may indicate instability or fear.
rules.professional_reasoning: The drawing's bounding box covers at most one fifth of the page. Fear or instability cannot be inferred without converging evidence.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing uses a small part of the page. Ask whether that was intentional and what the child chose to emphasize.
limitations: Age, motor skill, instructions, subject size, and available time may explain small scale.

rules.arabic: الرسم في أعلى الصفحة قد يعبر عن شخصية حالمة أو واقع خيالي وصعوبة في التكيف.
rules.english: The notes propose that drawing at the top indicates dreaminess, living in fantasy, and difficulty adapting.
rules.professional_reasoning: The foreground centroid is in the upper region. The supplied adaptation interpretation is not supported by the located research.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing is positioned high on the page; this is a spatial observation only.
limitations: Placement effects are weak, task-dependent, and not diagnostic.

rules.arabic: الرسم في الجانب الأيسر يدل على شخصية انطوائية.
rules.english: The notes propose that left-side placement indicates an introverted personality.
rules.professional_reasoning: The foreground centroid is left of centre. Introversion cannot be inferred from this placement.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing is positioned toward the left; this is a spatial observation only.
limitations: Handedness, script direction, composition, copying, and task constraints may influence placement.

rules.arabic: الرسم في الجانب الأيمن يدل على شخصية منفتحة.
rules.english: The notes propose that right-side placement indicates an outgoing personality.
rules.professional_reasoning: The foreground centroid is right of centre. Openness cannot be inferred from this placement.
rules.parent_safe_wording: The drawing is positioned toward the right; this is a spatial observation only.
limitations: Handedness, script direction, composition, copying, and task constraints may influence placement.
